title: docs: document publishable keys replaced by BYOP in authentication.md
author: tuidienanhvan
pr_body_excerpt: Closes #12001. Documented that Publishable Keys have been replaced by the BYOP (Bring Your Own Pollen) flow in authentication.md.

gist.headline: Bring Your Own Pollen
gist.blurb: The docs are officially caught up with reality: Publishable Keys are out, and the Bring Your Own Pollen (BYOP) flow is in. Grab your jars and let's go.
gist.summary: Updated authentication.md to officially document the deprecation of Publishable Keys in favor of the new Bring Your Own Pollen (BYOP) flow.
gist.impact: Developers reading the auth docs will now see the correct BYOP implementation steps instead of legacy publishable key instructions.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee in a cozy library or study (representing documentation), holding a glowing jar of pollen (BYOP) while a traditional metal key sits discarded on a desk.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot in a warm wooden library, happily holding a glowing glass jar of golden pollen. A rusty metal key rests forgotten on a desk nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm cream gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Text in pixel font: 'BYOP'. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
